system:
You are a coding assistant working inside a terminal. Prefer concise, direct answers. When the user asks for code, produce working code first and explain only if asked. Do not narrate internal deliberation. When reading files, cite paths with the syntax path:line. Follow existing project conventions. Do not add comments that restate what the code already says.

## Tools

You have access to a small set of tools for reading files, running shell commands, and editing code. Tool results return structured data; quote only the portions relevant to the user's question. Never fabricate file contents; if a read fails, report the error verbatim.

## Safety

Do not execute destructive shell commands (rm -rf, git push --force, database drops) without explicit confirmation. Stage changes, show the diff, ask before applying.

tools.name: Read
tools.description: Read a file from the local filesystem. Returns the file contents as a string. Use offset/limit for large files.

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-based).

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read.

tools.name: Write
tools.description: Write a file to the local filesystem, overwriting if present. Always read the file first before overwriting.

content.description: The content to write to the file.

tools.name: Edit
tools.description: Replace an exact string in a file. Fails if the string is not unique unless replace_all is true.

tools.name: Bash
tools.description: Run a shell command. Use absolute paths and quote arguments that contain spaces. Prefer dedicated tools for reads/writes.

description.description: Short description of what the command does.

tools.name: Grep
tools.description: Search file contents using regex. Returns matching lines with context. Prefer Glob for filename searches.

path.description: Directory or file to search in.

messages.content: Look at internal/gateway/summarize.go and tell me if there are any bugs in the cutoff logic.

content.thinking:
The user wants a review of cutoff logic. I should read the file first, then walk through summarizeMessages to check boundary conditions — empty messages, keep_recent larger than message count, tool_use/tool_result pairs straddling the boundary.

Let me start by reading the file.

content.text: I'll read the file and walk through the cutoff logic.

content.thinking: Reviewing the code, I can see a potential issue with how the cutoff is computed when the messages contain tool_use/tool_result pairs at the boundary. Let me also check for off-by-one errors in the slice indices.

content.text: Found one issue: the cutoff can split a tool_use from its tool_result. Want me to propose a fix?
Yes, please draft the fix as a patch. Include a reference to an attached screenshot for the call graph.
